title: dalbergia velutina var. verrucosa
intro: Dalbergia velutina var. verrucosa, also known as the Philippine ironwood, is a deciduous tree species native to the Philippines, characterized by its reddish-brown heartwood and distinctive verrucosa (wart-like) growths on its trunk. The medicinal properties of Philippine ironwood include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and wounds. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system, making it a valuable natural remedy for various ailments. Traditional herbal preparations of Philippine ironwood include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, although its usage should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
